docs.title: Systems Administrator & Cloud Support Engineer | Growing into SRE
The Story So Far
docs.text:
I’m currently working in Systems Administration and Cloud Support, but my head and heart are moving toward Site Reliability Engineering (SRE).

What makes my path a bit different? I spent years in Retail and Helpdesk before diving into deep infrastructure. That means I don’t just care if a server is "up"—I care about the person on the other side of the screen. I work to bridge the gap between complex cloud architecture and the actual user experience.

docs.title: The Foundations (Retail & Helpdesk)
docs.text:
• High-Pressure De-escalation: If you’ve handled an angry holiday crowd in retail, you can handle a P0 production outage without breaking a sweat.
  • Customer Engagement: I’ve learned to translate "tech-heavy" problems into simple solutions that make sense to everyone.

docs.title: Core Infrastructure (SysAdmin & Cloud)
docs.text:
• Systems: Years of managing Linux/Windows environments and keeping Active Directory healthy.
  • Cloud Operations: Building and scaling in AWS, Azure.

docs.title: The SRE Toolkit (Current Focus)
docs.text:
• Containers: Packaging apps with Docker and orchestrating with Kubernetes.
  • Incident Management: I don’t just fix things; I lead the response and write the Root Cause Analysis (RCA) to make sure it doesn't happen again.
  • Observability: Using Zabbix to see problems before the users do.

docs.title: Featured Work
docs.text:
See what I’ve been building lately:

  • [Project Name One]: A deep dive into automating cloud deployments with Terraform.
  • [Project Name Two]: Setting up a monitoring stack to track "Golden Signals" in K8s.
  • [Project Name Three]: Building a self-healing infrastructure script with Python.

View All Projects

docs.title: My Journey
docs.text:
Retail $\rightarrow$ IT Support $\rightarrow$ SysAdmin $\rightarrow$ Cloud Support $\rightarrow$ SRE

Each step has taught me that reliability is about more than just uptime—it’s about trust.

Let’s connect if you're looking to give me a job, a collaborating and learning opportunity or just to have conversation over chicken and chips..lol

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
Hey there! I'm Ifeanyi, and I've got a pretty interesting journey that's taken me from retail shelves to server rooms.

I spent 6 years in retail, working my way up from shelf attendant to store supervisor. But here's the thing—I was always the person my colleagues turned to when something went wrong with the computers. So after realizing that's where my real passion was, I went back to school and got my degree in Computer Science.

That led me to an educational organization where I started as an IT support intern, then moved into the helpdesk. Within a few months, I landed a full-time role as a system administrator—and I've been thriving there for the past 3 years.

So whether you're wondering why I switched careers or just want to chat about tech, IT infrastructure, or even retail stories, I'm here for it. All told, I've got 10 years of work experience under my belt, and I'm still learning something new every day.

docs.title: Get in Touch
docs.text:
Got a question? Want to collaborate? Just want to say hi? I'd love to hear from you.

  • Email: your.email@example.com
  • LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/yourprofile

Feel free to reach out anytime—I genuinely enjoy connecting with people!

docs.title: 1. Cloud Infrastructure Automation
docs.text:
Description: Designed and deployed production-grade infrastructure for an enterprise SaaS platform, with a focus on high availability, cost optimization, and security compliance.

Key Features: - Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with Terraform and AWS CloudFormation - CI/CD pipeline for automated provisioning and drift detection in GitHub Actions - Auto-scaling groups, VPC segmentation, and multi-AZ failover - Logging and alerts via CloudWatch, Datadog, and PagerDuty

Technologies & Tools: - - -

Links: - GitHub Repository - Architecture Diagram

Date: January 2025 – Present | Status: Ongoing

docs.title: 2. Enterprise Systems Administration & Cloud Support
docs.text:
Description: Managed and supported a hybrid data center + public cloud environment for a global application, focusing on reliability, incident response, and end-user support.

Key Features: - Windows and Linux systems deployment, patching, and configuration management (Ansible) - 24/7 incident coordination using ServiceNow and Slack - Capacity planning, cost monitoring, and rightsizing with AWS Cost Explorer and Azure Advisor - Security hardening, vulnerability scanning (Qualys), and SOC integration

Technologies & Tools: - - -

Links: - Support Runbook - Playbook Templates

Date: June 2024 – December 2025 | Status: Completed

docs.title: 3. Kubernetes Platform Operations
docs.text:
Description: Built and operated a production Kubernetes platform with GitOps deployment patterns and robust observability for DevOps teams.

Key Features: - EKS/GKE cluster management, node pool autoscaling, and service mesh adoption (Istio) - Helm charts, Argo CD, and GitOps workflows for environment consistency - Monitoring with Prometheus, Grafana dashboards, and alerting with Opsgenie

Technologies & Tools: - - -

Links: - K8s Operations Guide - Cluster Auto-remediation Scripts

Date: March 2023 – November 2024 | Status: Completed
